name: propose-shipment-labels
description:
Propose source tags to be added to the case.
BEFORE proposing, use query-case-data with model="source_tag" to check existing tags and avoid duplicates.
Source tags are independent objects — removing a tag from sources does not delete it, and bulk edits never
destroy tags. Only explicit deletion removes a tag.
MANDATORY: Your response MUST only acknowledge completion - do not repeat, summarize, or list the proposed
source tags as they display automatically in the UI.
- Do not include citations in the tag descriptions - write clear, standalone descriptions.

name: describe-fleet-data-model
description:
REQUIRED before query-case-data. You MUST call this tool
for every model you intend to query BEFORE calling
query-case-data. It returns the exact filter names, field
names, sort options, and query builder fields. Without
this information, query-case-data calls will fail.

Call this for EVERY model mentioned or implied by the
user's request. For example, if the user asks about
"sources with many citations", describe both "source" and
"citation" — parent models often have aggregated fields
(like citation_count on source) that you would otherwise
miss.

name: query-fleet-data
description:
PREREQUISITE: Before calling this tool, you MUST call
describe-case-data-model for EVERY model you intend to
query. Do NOT skip this step — without it you will not
know the valid filter names, field names, or sort options
and your query WILL fail. If you have not yet called
describe-case-data-model for this model in this
conversation, stop and call it first.

Query structured case data by model type. Returns filtered,
paginated results with a total count.

**Pagination:** Results are paginated (default 25, max 200).
Use limit/offset for most models. For sources, use
cursor-based pagination: pass back "next_cursor" as
"cursor" to get the next page.

**Query builder:** Sources and facts support advanced
filtering via the "query" parameter — an array of filter
objects with field, lookup, value, and optional
invert/conjoined. Call describe-case-data-model to see
available query fields and lookups.

Available models:
- source: Documents and files (cursor-based pagination)
- source_tag: Tags applied to sources
- case_contact: Contacts linked to this case
- issue: Legal issues
- claim: Legal claims (groups of issues)
- task: Case tasks
- fact: Timeline facts (accepted only)
- fact_note: Notes on facts
- citation: Links between facts and sources
- designation: Deposition designations
- task_note: Notes on tasks
- draft: Draft documents
- upload: File uploads
- conversation: AI conversations (yours only)
- conversation_message: Messages in conversations (preview only; use read-conversation-message for full content)

For full-text search with wildcards, fuzzy matching, or
boolean operators across multiple record types, use
case-search instead.

name: search-shipments-keyword
description:
Search for documents and sources using keyword matching. This tool searches specifically
through document content, names, memos, titles, and other source metadata using keyword
and phrase matching. Use this when you need to find specific documents, files, or sources
by searching for terms that appear in the document content or metadata. The search uses
Elasticsearch query string syntax for advanced searches including wildcards, phrase matching,
and boolean operators. The benefit of this tool is that it allows you to search ALL sources
and not only those indexed for semantic search.

IMPORTANT: Content highlights include a 'location' field which typically represents a zero-indexed
page number (page 1 = location "0", page 2 = location "1", etc.). In some cases, this location may
represent something other than pages, such as timestamps or spreadsheet sections.
